mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS declines but 20-day forward returns in 60%+ of cases are negative or flat (t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Elevated unemployment rates (UNRATE > 5%) signal weak labor market conditions that depress corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE > 5% but 20-day forward returns exceed 1.5% in 50%+ of cases (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust economic demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through improved corporate revenue prospects.
failure_condition: GDPC1 and HOUST both rise but 60-day returns are negative in 40%+ of cases (Rank IC < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO > 110) reflects strong manufacturing activity, which boosts corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO > 110 but 20-day returns are negative in 30%+ of cases (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%), monetary tightening (FEDFUNDS > 4%) compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS > 4% but 20-day returns are positive in 50%+ of cases (Rank IC > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 100k) signals a tightening labor market that increases corporate hiring costs and reduces earnings visibility, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS > 100k but 5-day returns exceed 1% in 40%+ of cases (t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing permits (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strong consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days through improved corporate revenue outlooks.
failure_condition: HOUST and RSAFS both rise but 20-day returns are negative in 35%+ of cases (Rank IC < 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects stable supply chain conditions that support corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth but 60-day returns are negative in 50%+ of cases (t-test p > 0.05).
